newsSinceJun6: No material company-specific news since June 6. Only fresh disclosure is a routine weekly share-buyback report (May 28 - June 3). Pre-window context remains the driver set: NVIDIA Omniverse DSX Blueprint integration (June 1), pending SoftBank robotics sale, and Q1 Electrification orders +44% with triple-digit data-center order growth. Today's -2.61% (CHF 80.52, ~6% below the May 27 all-time high of CHF 85.38) has no identifiable fundamental trigger and looks flow/rotation-driven.

sources.label: Yahoo Finance - ABB expands NVIDIA collaboration (DSX Blueprint, June 1, 2026)
Yahoo Finance - ABB Reshapes AI Data Center Focus As Robotics Move Nears SoftBank
TradingView/EQS - ABB share buybacks May 28 - June 3, 2026

verdicts.rankRationale: Hold: no new evidence since June 6 on a crowding-sensitive list; ABB stays a well-owned $189B benchmark name far from the passive/power bottleneck core, and a -2.6% drift does not change residual alpha at rank 56.
verdicts.bull: Electrification is the structural winner inside ABB: +44% order growth with triple-digit data-center orders, and the NVIDIA DSX integration embeds ABB gear in AI build-out design flows.
verdicts.neutral: Everything bullish here is already consensus and in the +31% YTD price; the name functions as a sector benchmark, not hidden alpha.
verdicts.bear: Mega-cap crowding plus a rerated multiple means any data-center order air pocket or grid capex pause hits a stock priced near all-time highs.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: Q2 orders confirming continued triple-digit data-center electrification growth
verdicts.primaryRisk: Crowded ownership unwinding on any deceleration in AI/data-center capex

verdicts.rankRationale: Hold: this page explicitly discounts large, heavily covered names, and nothing since June 6 makes ABB more latent; it remains too big and too explicitly AI-linked for early discovery at rank 95.
verdicts.bull: Power & Grid exposure is genuine and deepening - switchgear, UPS and distribution content per MW keeps rising as AI densities climb.
verdicts.neutral: Strategic relevance is intact but fully mapped by the street; low-conviction tail position on a latency-weighted list is correct.
verdicts.bear: Post-robotics-divestiture ABB is an even purer consensus electrification proxy, i.e. the opposite of a latent node.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: Closing of the SoftBank robotics sale refocusing capital onto grid/electrification
verdicts.primaryRisk: Zero discovery premium left; performance now depends on execution, not recognition

verdicts.rankRationale: Hold with mild negative drift: no new robotics news since June 6, but the nearing SoftBank divestiture keeps eroding ABB's robotics purity, so its claim on a robotics list weakens structurally rather than on fresh evidence.
verdicts.bull: Until the sale closes, ABB still owns a top-tier industrial robotics franchise and the spin/sale process can surface value embedded in the parent.
verdicts.neutral: ABB is a great robotics operator but a poor robotics-alpha vehicle; the exposure is being sold to SoftBank, not compounded inside ABB.
verdicts.bear: Once robotics transfers to SoftBank, ABB holders retain no upside to the humanoid/cobot theme this page tracks, making the listing close to obsolete.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: Final terms/closing of the ABB Robotics sale to SoftBank
verdicts.primaryRisk: Listing becomes stale as robotics exposure exits the listed entity

verdicts.rankRationale: Hold: yesterday's down move already paid the rerating penalty; today's -2.6% no-news pullback (~6% off the May 27 high) with the electrification-bottleneck thesis fully intact marginally improves asymmetry but is not enough to reverse rank.
verdicts.bull: Grid interconnection, transformers, switchgear and data-center electrical content are scarce bottleneck capacity; +44% Electrification orders and the NVIDIA DSX design-flow tie-in argue the backlog keeps building.
verdicts.neutral: Real bottleneck exposure diluted across a $189B conglomerate; +31% YTD means the market already pays for it, so the pullback only restores modest, not asymmetric, upside.
verdicts.bear: If hyperscaler electrical capex digests in H2, a stock rerated to record highs on data-center orders de-rates faster than its backlog declines.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: Continued record Electrification orders and post-divestiture margin-target uplift
verdicts.primaryRisk: Rerated multiple meeting any slowdown in data-center electrical order intake
